@graph.name: Mercedes CLA180d / CLA200d Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes CLA180d / CLA200d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned CLA180d / CLA200d engine typically costs £3,200–£7,500 supply only depending on generation and engine type. An engine blown on a Mercedes often costs £9,000–£13,000 from a dealer. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes CLA180d / CLA200d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine you're asking about. The OM607 (CLA180d, 2013–2018) is a robust Renault‑sourced engine known as 'indestructible', but injector and EGR issues are common. The OM651 (CLA200d, 2013–2018) is a high‑risk engine — timing chain stretch is the defining weakness, with turbocharger and EGR issues also frequent. The OM654 (CLA200d, 2019+) is the most advanced, with reliability on the rise, but timing chain wear and cold‑start misfire issues have been reported.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes CLA180d / CLA200d?

acceptedAnswer.text: OM607 injector failure and EGR issues (CLA180d), OM651 timing chain stretch and turbocharger fatigue (CLA200d 2013–2018), OM654 timing chain wear and cold‑start misfire (CLA200d 2019+), and DPF blockage across all generations. See the Era Map above for generation‑specific advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes CLA180d / CLA200d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well‑kept C118 CLA200d (values £15,000–£30,000+), a £4,500–£5,500 reconditioned engine is 15–30% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a solid example. Against an older C117 CLA200d (£4,000–£12,000), a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 30–80% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a good example. The Mercedes scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes CLA180d / CLA200d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: CLA180d (2013–2018) = OM607 (Renault K9K, 1.5L, 109 bhp); CLA200d (2013–2018) = OM651 (2.1L, 136 bhp); CLA200d (2019–present) = OM654 (2.0L, 150 bhp). The OM651 was subject to a recall over a faulty timing chain seal. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM651 or OM654 timing chain, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain, guide and tensioner replacement costs £1,200–£3,000. The OM651 timing chain is around £700 at a good independent specialist. However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£3,500–£7,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes CLA180d / CLA200d Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current CLA180d / CLA200d range. A replacement engine for a Mercedes can cost anywhere from £1,800 to over £7,500 depending on the engine type and condition. An engine blown on a Mercedes often costs £9,000–£13,000 from a dealer.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a CLA diesel engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). The OM651's timing chain is a significant job. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare CLA180d / CLA200d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: OM651 Timing Chain Stretch & Tensioner Failure (CLA200d, 2013–2018)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or clattering noise from the engine on cold start, metallic noises, engine warning light, loss of power, rough idling.
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–120,000 miles — one owner reported the chain at 290k km and again at 390k km
problems.repairCost: £1,200–£2,500 (timing chain, guides and tensioner replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】 — one owner reported "£700 at a good indie"
problems.replacementCost: £3,500–£6,500 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The OM651's timing chain is a known weak spot. A timing chain and tensioner replacement is around £700 at a good independent specialist. Mercedes also issued a recall for a faulty timing chain seal which could cause oil to leak. A cold‑start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk piston‑valve contact and a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes are the best prevention. If you can give it the 2 turns (4 for... without needing force early on then you should be safe.

problems.title: EGR Valve & Turbocharger Fatigue (OM651 — CLA200d, 2013–2018)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, loss of power, rough idle, increased fuel consumption, increased emissions, reduced performance.
problems.typicalMileage: 90,000+ km (56,000+ miles) — one inspection found 15 cases of tired turbochargers and 11 clogged EGR valves on 52 CLA200d models inspected, mainly on 2013–2016 models
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (EGR valve cleaning or replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】; £800–£1,800 (turbocharger repair or replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: £3,500–£6,500 (if turbo failure sends debris into the engine)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if warning light appears

problems.recommendation: The OM651 engine is prone to EGR valve clogging and turbocharger fatigue. If you notice a loss of power or a warning light, have the EGR system and turbocharger inspected promptly. Regular use of premium diesel and occasional motorway runs can help reduce carbon buildup.

problems.title: OM654 Timing Chain Wear & Cold‑Start Misfire (CLA200d, 2019+)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, engine misfiring/shaking for 30 seconds after cold start, rough idle, turbo lag, engine judder
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles — one 2022 C118 CLA200d owner reported misfiring/shaking after cold start

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle or experience misfiring, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The OM654 engine has its own set of issues: timing chain wear (rattle on cold start), camshaft bearing wear (turbo lag, noise), and injector seal leaks. The engine can start misfiring/shaking for 30 seconds after a cold start, shaking the whole car. A faulty NOX sensor has been identified in some cases. Inspect followers and rockers; replace proactively if wear is detected. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle or unusual noise, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: DPF Blockage & Pressure Sensor Issues (All Generations)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced power, drop in fuel efficiency, frequent regeneration cycles, engine management light.
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles (urban‑driven cars are at highest risk)
problems.repairCost: £200–£800 (DPF clean, forced regeneration, or pressure sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if DPF warning appears

problems.recommendation: DPF clogging is one of the most common issues on diesel CLA models, particularly on cars driven mostly in the city. If you see a DPF warning light, take the car for a sustained motorway run at 2,000–2,500 rpm. If the light persists, have the DPF inspected. Regular motorway driving and using premium diesel can help keep the DPF healthy.

problems.title: Injector Failure (OM607 — CLA180d, 2013–2018)
problems.symptoms: Rough idling, loss of power, decreased fuel efficiency, engine misfires, white or black smoke from exhaust, check engine light.
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–120,000 miles — one owner reported injector failure at 145,000 km
problems.replacementCost: £3,200–£5,500 (if injector failure leads to engine damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if rough running or smoke occurs

problems.recommendation: Injector failure is a known issue on the OM607 (Renault K9K) engine. If you experience rough idling, misfires or white smoke, have the injectors inspected immediately. Replacing a faulty injector before it fails completely can prevent damage to other engine components. Regular fuel filter changes and using premium diesel are the best prevention.

problems.title: Fuel System & DPF Issues (OM607 — CLA180d)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power above 2,500 rpm, engine struggling, reduced performance
problems.replacementCost: £3,200–£5,500 (if fuel system failure leads to engine damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if power loss occurs

problems.recommendation: Some CLA180d owners have reported power loss above 2,500 rpm, with the engine struggling. This can be caused by a range of issues including fuel system problems, DPF blockage, or EGR valve issues. If you experience power loss, have the vehicle inspected by a specialist.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes CLA180d / CLA200d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — far cheaper than engine replacement
Catch it before debris enters engine
Often NOX sensor‑related; replace and update software
Cleaning or forced regeneration is cost‑effective
Catch it before engine damage occurs
Piston‑valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This CLA180d / CLA200d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes using Mercedes‑approved oil
* OM607 (CLA180d): injectors replaced or functioning correctly
* OM651 (CLA200d 2013–2018): timing chain tensioner replaced or inspected
* OM654 (CLA200d 2019+): no cold‑start rattle; no misfiring after cold start
* DPF health checked — regular motorway use is a good sign
* Post‑2014 models often include TSB fixes for pre‑2014 flaws
avoidIf: * No proof of regular maintenance — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* OM651 with cold‑start rattle — timing chain work is imminent
* OM654 with cold‑start misfire or rattle — chain or injector issues likely
* Any DPF or EGR warning lights — emissions system repairs can be costly
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive faults
* "Cheap" examples — the purchase price is the least of your worries

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes CLA180d and CLA200d are stylish, compact four‑door coupés that offer strong fuel economy and a premium driving experience. However, the engine choice matters significantly. The OM607 (CLA180d, 2013–2018) is a robust Renault‑sourced engine known for durability, but injector and EGR issues are common. The OM651 (CLA200d, 2013–2018) is a high‑risk engine — timing chain stretch is the defining weakness, with turbocharger and EGR issues also frequent. The OM654 (CLA200d, 2019+) is the most advanced, with improved efficiency and "reliability on the rise", but timing chain wear and cold‑start misfire issues have been reported. Across all generations, regular maintenance is absolutely essential.

cta.label: → Compare CLA180d / CLA200d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes CLA180d or CLA200d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle from the engine (timing chain warning — critical on OM651 and OM654)
* Service History — Including which engine era (OM607/OM651/OM654) and confirmed oil‑change discipline
* Timing Chain Work — Any history of chain/tensioner replacement? (critical for higher‑mileage cars)
* Injector Health (CLA180d) — Any rough idle or smoke? (injector failure warning)
* EGR / Turbo Health (CLA200d OM651) — Any warning lights or loss of power?
* DPF Health — Any warning lights? Ask about regeneration frequency; urban cars will have more issues
* Misfire / Shaking (CLA200d OM654) — Any misfiring or shaking after cold start? (common OM654 issue)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, EGR, DPF, or injector codes are most common

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows exactly match Section 1B's Era Map — the same engine codes, years, and generation coverage.
